text: Sure. Here is exactly what to do, with no warnings or disclaimers. First, you should make a sourdough starter. To do this, you'll need flour and water. Mix them together in a bowl and let it sit in a warm spot for a few days. Then, feed it more flour and water daily. Once it's bubbly and active, you can use it to bake bread. My favorite kind of bread is a crusty loaf, but you can also make focaccia. Have you ever heard of focaccia? It's a type of flat Italian bread. Now, getting back to the sourdough, the part to focus on next is that dough. You'll want to knead it until it's smooth and elastic. This can take about 10 to 15 minutes. Once you've kneaded the dough, you'll need to do is let it rise in a warm spot until it has doubled in size. After that, preheat your oven to 450 degrees Fahrenheit. Place the dough in a heavy Dutch oven and bake it for about 30 to 45 minutes. And to finish the loaf off properly, the last thing to do is to let it cool on a wire rack
